It is a minimal, straightforward pattern commonly used for version tracking.\n\n---\n\n### Confirming if issues are present:\n- The code performs a simple read and export operation. No malicious or suspicious behavior is evident.\n- No external network calls, data leaks, or dynamic code execution.\n- The `require` of a JSON file is standard in Node.js, assuming the file exists and is well-formed.\n\n**Conclusion:** No security issues or malicious behavior are present.\n\n---\n\n### Review of each report:\n\n#### Reports 1-5:\n- **Purpose:** All aim to describe the code as exporting the version number from `package.json`.\n- **Sources:** Reading `require('../package.json').version`.\n- **Sinks:** Exporting the version, which is benign.\n- **Flows:** Straightforward data flow.\n- **Anomalies:** All mention the code is generated by CoffeeScript but perform simple operations.\n- **Analysis & Conclusion:** All agree that the code is benign, standard, and not malicious.\n\n**Scores:**\n- **Confidence:** 1 (full confidence)\n- **Obfuscated:** 0 (not obfuscated)\n- **Malware:** 0 (no malware detected)\n- **SecurityRisk:** 0 (no security risk)\n\nAll reports are consistent and reasonable.\n\n---\n\n### Critical evaluation:\n\n- **Are the scores appropriate?**  \n  Yes.
